title: Sugar cookies
subtitle: American cut-out biscuits for decorating
excerpt: Plain, lightly vanilla-scented American cut-out cookies that hold their shape cleanly through rolling and baking. The base for royal-iced and decorated biscuits, or good as they are with a glass of milk. Makes about twenty-four.
sourceNotes: Sugar cookies as American cut-out biscuits are documented in nineteenth-century US domestic cookbooks. Lettice Bryan, The Kentucky Housewife (1839), public domain: sugar biscuits made with flour, butter, and sugar, rolled and cut into shapes, are recorded as a standard American domestic preparation. The USDA Cooperative Extension Service baking publications document the cut-out sugar cookie as one of the most widely reproduced American domestic biscuit recipes, noting the importance of chilling before rolling to prevent shape loss.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked cut-out shapes in a single layer, then bag once solid, for up to 2 months. Bake from frozen, adding 2 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the batch and bake on two trays.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough keeps wrapped in the fridge for 3 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; pale gold at the edge with a set but not-yet-firm centre — they firm on the tray

content.text: A sugar cookie that holds its shape through baking needs a firm, cold dough with no excess liquid. The baking powder is minimal and the proportion of butter to flour lower than shortbread; both keep the spread in check. Chilling after cutting (5 minutes in the freezer is faster than 30 in the fridge) produces crisper edges from the cut.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale and creamy. Add the {{eggs}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and beat until combined. Add the {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} and mix until a smooth dough forms; stop as soon as the flour is incorporated. Divide into two flat discs, wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 175°C fan. Roll one disc on a lightly floured surface to 5 mm thick. Cut shapes with floured cutters, place on lined trays 3 cm apart, and refrigerate for 10 minutes before baking. Bake for 9–11 minutes until pale gold at the edges with a set but still slightly soft centre. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes to firm before transferring to a rack.
